hero.title: LinkedIn Guide for DevOps Engineers
hero.subtitle: Optimize your profile to attract tech recruiters and engineering managers.

sections.title: Professional Banner
sections.content: Use a banner that reflects your technical focus, such as cloud architecture diagrams or a clean, modern tech-themed background.

sections.title: Profile Photo Tips
sections.content: Ensure clear lighting, a neutral background, and professional attire. A friendly smile goes a long way.

sections.title: Headline Examples
sections.content: Instead of just 'DevOps Engineer', try: 'DevOps Engineer | AWS & Kubernetes | Building Scalable Infrastructure' or 'Cloud Engineer helping teams ship faster with CI/CD'.

sections.title: About Me Examples
sections.content: Tell your story. Explain *why* you love infrastructure and automation. Include a summary of your core tech stack and the types of problems you enjoy solving.

sections.title: Experience Examples
sections.content: Write your experience like your resume, focusing on impact. Link to company pages and attach media (like architecture diagrams or project links) where possible.

sections.title: Skills Section
sections.content: Pin your top 3 technical skills (e.g., Kubernetes, Terraform, AWS). Endorsements matter less than passing LinkedIn Skill Assessments.

sections.title: Networking Tips
sections.content: Connect with recruiters, join DevOps groups, and comment thoughtfully on industry posts. Post about what you are currently learning or building.

sections.title: LinkedIn Optimization for Recruiters
sections.content: Turn on 'Open to Work'. Ensure your 'Location' is accurate (or set to where you want to work), and use standard job titles so recruiters can find you in search.

beforeAfter.after: Cloud & DevOps Engineer | AWS Certified | Automating Deployments
